characters.name: 63-D94A
characters.description: 63-D94AAliasesOrganizational NamesAsset 63-D94A[1]SpeciesInhumanCitizenshipAmerican(formerly)CanadianGenderFemaleStatusAliveAppearancesTV SeriesAgents of S.H.I.E.L.D.*BOOM(mentioned) Asset63-D94Ais anInhuman. ThisInhumanhad powers that related to the cold. She was recognized byS.H.I.E.L.D., who gave her the name of 63-D94A for privacy purposes. When she found out Inhumans were in danger,Daisy Johnsonhad 63-D94A sent toCalgaryfor protection as well as for training her powers in cold climates.[1]

characters.name: A'Lars
characters.description: A'LarsReal NameA'Lars[1]SpeciesTitanGenderMaleAffiliationTitan Royal FamilyStatusUnknownAppearancesMoviesAvengers: Infinity War(mentioned)ComicsAvengers: Endgame Prelude(mentioned) A'Larsis the father ofThanosand the adoptive father ofEros. A'Lars lived on the planetTitanand was the head of theTitan Royal Family. He had a relationship with a woman and together had a son that they namedThanos. Later, they adopted anEternalnamedEros.[2]Over time, Eros left Titan and Thanos warned of the threat of overpopulation and suggested a solution in the form of genocide. He eventually left and became a galactic warlord, while Titan was overcome by overpopulation and following a catastrophic event, was left uninhabited.[1]

characters.name: A'Lars | Earth-351
characters.description: A'LarsReal NameA'Lars[1]SpeciesTitanGenderMaleAffiliationTitan Royal FamilyStatusAliveAppearancesGamesWhat If...? – An Immersive Story(mentioned) A'Larsis the father ofThanosand the adoptive father ofEros. When his sonThanoswas arrested for attempting to steal theReality Stone, A'Lars sent his adoptive sonErosto advocate for his brother and inform theCouncil of Titanthat A'Lars would be willing to bribe them.[2]

characters.name: A. Coulter
characters.description: A. CoulterReal NameA. Coulter[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleAffiliationStark IndustriesStatusAliveAppearancesTV SeriesAgents of S.H.I.E.L.D.*0-8-4A. Coulteris an employee ofStark Industriesand designer of theBus. Part of theStark Industriesteam tasked with building a mobile command center forS.H.I.E.L.D.Aviation Command, A. Coulter designed theCXD 23215 Airborne Mobile Command Station. Coulter's name was included on the preliminary schematics for the aircraft, copies of which was later kept inPhil Coulson's office on the aircraft itself, and later in the director's office in theLighthouse.[1][2][3]

characters.name: A. Kennedy
characters.description: A. KennedyReal NameA. Kennedy[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleTitle(s)OfficerAffiliationRiver's End Police DepartmentStatusAliveAppearancesTV SeriesAgents of S.H.I.E.L.D.*All the Comforts of Home*The Real Deal(mentioned)Actors/ActressesPortrayed byAntonio AaronOfficerA. Kennedyis an officer of theRiver's End Police Department. While driving his police car Kennedy spotted a familiar van on the other side of the crossroad. He used his alarm to make the van stop and noticed it being driven byAlphonso MackenzieandPhil Coulson. When asked if there was a problem Kennedy told Mackenzie to tell him. At first Mackenzie thought Kennedy was talking about his bruises. Kennedy told him that this was not the case, but that he was talking about the van.John Nickelhad been trying to sell this van for months, and now with someone else driving the van Kennedy wondered if it was a clunker or not and how badly Mackenzie was fleeced on the deal. Mackenzie started laughing, telling Kennedy that he had been looking for a van like it for ages, and that the price was a steal. This surprised Kennedy, after which he wished Mackenzie and Coulson a great day.[1] Later that evening, following the arrest of a drunkenDeke Shaw, Kennedy was sitting behind his desk at the police station. WhenDaisy Johnsonentered the station to free Shaw, Kennedy listened to the conversation she had withC. Wellins. He eventually recognized Johnson as an agent ofS.H.I.E.L.D.contacted the military. In order to gain more information, Kennedy stopped Quake and Shaw from leaving the police station, telling them to fill in some paperwork. He also asked Quake for a number so he could reach her for a follow up. After giving the information Quake and Shaw left the police station.[1]When the military arrived Wellins told them that he did not recognize them, but that Kennedy, one of his officers, thought there was quite a resemblance.[2]

At first Mackenzie thought Kennedy was talking about his bruises. Kennedy told him that this was not the case, but that he was talking about the van.John Nickelhad been trying to sell this van for months, and now with someone else driving the van Kennedy wondered if it was a clunker or not and how badly Mackenzie was fleeced on the deal. Mackenzie started laughing, telling Kennedy that he had been looking for a van like it for ages, and that the price was a steal. This surprised Kennedy, after which he wished Mackenzie and Coulson a great day.[1] Later that evening, following the arrest of a drunkenDeke Shaw, Kennedy was sitting behind his desk at the police station. WhenDaisy Johnsonentered the station to free Shaw, Kennedy listened to the conversation she had withC. Wellins. He eventually recognized Johnson as an agent ofS.H.I.E.L.D.contacted the military. In order to gain more information, Kennedy stopped Quake and Shaw from leaving the police station, telling them to fill in some paperwork. He also asked Quake for a number so he could reach her for a follow up. After giving the information Quake and Shaw left the police station.[1]When the military arrived Wellins told them that he did not recognize them, but that Kennedy, one of his officers, thought there was quite a resemblance.[2]

characters.name: A. Thomas
characters.description: A. ThomasReal NameA. Thomas[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleTitle(s)WriterAffiliationThe Dallas RecordStatusAliveAppearancesTV SeriesHelstrom*Mother's Little Helpers(mentioned)A. Thomaswas a writer ofThe Dallas Recordand did a piece on the murder ofRebecca Torres. Hearing aboutRebecca Torres' murder, Thomas decided to write a story about it forThe Dallas Record. He eventually had interviews with Torres' parents as well as the police department, but was unable to get valid evidence, annoying him.[1]

characters.name: A.D. Doug
characters.description: A.D. DougReal NameA.D. Doug[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleTitle(s)Ph.D.AffiliationCalifornia Institute of TechnologyStatusAliveAppearancesTV SeriesLoki*Lamentis(indirectly mentioned)*The Nexus Event(indirectly mentioned)A.D. Doug, Ph.D. is an author and physics professor atCalifornia Institute of Technology. A.D. Doug was an unsuccessful writer inPasadena,California. During the1990s, he lived in a residence with a giant lab room that he worked in while also juggling a job as a professor of theoretical physics.[2]

characters.name: A.D. Doug | A.D. Doug's Real Life
characters.description: A.D. DougReal NameA.D. Doug[1]AliasesNicknamesO.B.[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleDate of DeathMid-1990s(reversed)[1]Mid-1990s[2]Title(s)Ph.D.AffiliationCalifornia Institute of Technology(formerly)StatusDeceasedAppearancesTV SeriesLoki*Science/FictionActors/ActressesPortrayed byKe Huy Quan A.D. Doug, Ph.D. was a writer and theoretical physicist. A.D. Doug was a part-time writer on a branched timeline who lived inPasadena,California. He became a scientist and taught theoretical physics atCaltech, and worked to earn a Ph.D. so that he could keep his job. However, he aspired to become a full-time writer. He eventually got married to a woman.

characters.name: A.I.M. President
characters.description: A.I.M. PresidentSpeciesHumanCitizenshipBritishEnglishGenderMaleAffiliationA.I.M.StatusUnknownAppearancesGamesIron ManActors/ActressesVoiced byFred Tatasciore TheA.I.M. Presidentwas the CEO ofAdvanced Idea MechanicsuntilIron Mandestroyed the company as a result of their deals withObadiah Stane. TheA.I.M.president was called byStark IndustriesCOO,Obadiah Staneamidst an ongoing weapons war with theMaggiaandTen Rings. Stane offered for them to merge to become stronger. The president liked this idea and asked whatTony Starkthought, but Stane assured him Stark was retiring.

characters.name: A.I.M. President | A.I.M. Operative Controller
characters.description: A.I.M. PresidentSpeciesHumanCitizenshipBritishEnglishGenderMaleAffiliationA.I.M.StatusUnknownAppearancesGamesIron ManActors/ActressesVoiced byFred Tatasciore TheA.I.M. Presidentwas the CEO ofAdvanced Idea MechanicsuntilIron Mandestroyed the company as a result of their deals withObadiah Stane. The president and Stane often talked over the phone for business purposes as Stane wanted his own armor, but became impatient at A.I.M.'s progress on it. Obadiah Stanecontacted the A.I.M. President to broker a merger. He insisted it would be wise with bothStark IndustriesandA.I.M.coming under attack from groups such as theMaggia. The A.I.M. President asked aboutTony Stark, whom he considered a "billionaire brat," and Stane advised him not to worry as Stark was entering the philanthropic phase of his life. With Stane promising that a merger would make both companies stronger, more profitable and safe from outside influences, the A.I.M. President expressed interest to go through with the alliance.

characters.name: Aadam
characters.description: AadamReal NameAadam[1]CitizenshipNoor Dimension(formerly)GenderMaleDate of DeathFall 2025[2][1][3][4]AffiliationClandestinesStatusDeceasedAppearancesTV SeriesMs. Marvel*Destined*Seeing RedActors/ActressesPortrayed byAli Alsaleh Aadamwas a member of theClandestines. Under the orders ofNajma, he attempted to steal one of theQuantum BandsfromKamala Khan, only to be killed in battle byRed Dagger. Like the otherClandestines, Aadam was exiled from theNoor Dimensionand trapped on theEarthly Plane. In1942, he and the other Clandestines journeyed toIndiain an attempt to find theQuantum Bandsthat would help them return home. Aadam stood watch while the others looked for the bangle, and he alerted them of the arrival of theBritish Armed Forcesthat started to destroy the ruins they were searching. Aadam thus fled with the other Clandestines, minusAishawho had left on her own and would not be found byNajmabefore several years.[1]

characters.name: Aamir Khan
characters.description: Aamir KhanReal NameAamir Khan(عامر خان)[1]SpeciesHumanHybridCitizenshipPakistaniAmericanGenderMaleDate of BirthLate 1990s-Early 2000s[2]AffiliationColes Academic High School(formerly)StatusAliveAppearancesMoviesThe MarvelsTV SeriesMs. Marvel(4 episodes)Daredevil: Born Again(photo)Actors/ActressesPortrayed bySaagar Shaikh Aamir Khan(Urdu:عامر خان) is the older brother ofKamala Khan. Aamir was the first child ofYusufandMuneeba Khan, and was born inKarachi,Pakistan. After moving toJersey Cityin theUnited States of Americaas a young child, his parents initially struggled to earn a decent living.[3]Aamir later had a sister,Kamala, with whom he had a loving relationship, and attendedColes Academic High School.[4]During his youth, he had a time during which he wore a lot of black clothes, which his family jokingly referred to as his "goth phase".[5]Aamir was also noticeable for being the most religious member of the family.[1]

characters.name: Aaron (Apple Inc.)
characters.description: AaronReal NameAaron[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleAffiliationApple Inc.StatusAliveAppearancesMoviesCaptain America: The Winter SoldierActors/ActressesPortrayed byD.C. Pierson Aaronis an employee of theApple Inc.store who worked at a shopping mall inWashington, D.C. Working at an Apple store, Aaron approached a couple of customers who were working at the store's computers and asked if they needed any assistance. Thewomantold him and they were engaged and looked at honeymoon locations, to which Aaron congratulated them. Aaron asked them if they had found a vacation spot and the couple looked at the monitor and said it wasNew Jersey.  Aaron then looked closer at themanand remarked that he owned a similar pair of eyeglasses, to which the woman jokingly said that they were identical.[1]

characters.name: Aaron (Baby)
characters.description: AaronReal NameAaron[1]SpeciesHumanCitizenshipAmericanGenderMaleStatusAliveAppearancesTV SeriesJessica Jones*AKA The Octopus Aaronis the son ofSophieandMarcus. Sophiewalked with her son cradled in an attempt to get him to stop crying; walking pastKarl Malus' Residence, she was elated that the song on the piano calmed him. She introduced herself toAlisa Jonesand asked to sit inside while she played, to which Jones reluctantly agreed. As Jones continued to lose concentration to the crying, Sophie questioned if the wails were bothering her. Despite attempting to calm her son, she became frightened by the shift in an uneasy symphony, storming out of the house.[1]
